name: BubR1 overexpression extended healthy lifespan in mice
summary: The strongest direct genome-surveillance intervention signal is transgenic BubR1 overexpression in mice.
statement: Sustained high BubR1 expression preserved genomic integrity, reduced tumorigenesis, delayed age-related deterioration and aneuploidy in several tissues, and extended lifespan in mice.
population_or_model: Transgenic BubR1-overexpressing mice and related aneuploidy- or tumor-prone mouse models
time_horizon: Lifespan and age-related tissue analyses in mice
quantitative_note: The abstract reports extended lifespan and delayed age-related deterioration, with protection against oncogene-induced aneuploidization and cancer.
caveats: The intervention is a transgenic overexpression strategy, not a clinically deployable therapy.
Mouse lifespan and tumor-protection effects may not translate to safe human checkpoint manipulation.
The findings do not establish benefit from small molecules or transient modulation in older humans.
